journal_entry.description: Meal purchase on corporate credit card
journal_entry.rationale: Cannot determine correct account without knowing business purpose. Meals can be: (1) Meals and Entertainment (50% deductible client/business development meals), (2) Employee Benefits (staff meals, taxable benefit), (3) Travel Expenses (meals during business travel), (4) Office Expenses (working meals during meetings), or (5) Other categories. Each has different tax treatment and account classification under IFRS. The transaction text provides only the amount and payment method, not the business context needed to select the appropriate expense account.
clarification_questions: What was the business purpose of this meal? (e.g., client entertainment, employee working meal, team meeting, travel meal, etc.)

output.reason: Corporate credit card used = liability increase (credit card payable increases by $125)

credit_classifier.reason: Corporate credit card used = liability increase (credit card payable increases by $125)

output.reason: Meal expense = expense increase. The $125 meal is a business expense (meals & entertainment), which is increased by debiting.
